title: 顺水推舟
summary:
Beatrice informs Rolly that she overheard a significant conversation while in Room 4 at the Stagg. After receiving this information, Rolly decides to seek legal counsel from his uncle, Jeremy Claude, realizing the details involve the entire Claude family. Rolly travels to Jeremy’s house on High Street and waits in the study to maintain confidentiality, rather than joining Jeremy and Francis for dinner. While waiting, Rolly observes several items in the study, including a briefcase belonging to the late Sir William Jessamy and photographs of Francis, Lord Edward Trenton, and Jeremy's deceased son, Anthony. In the dining room, Francis and Jeremy discuss Rolly's visit; Francis expresses suspicion regarding the relationship between Rolly and Lynn. When they eventually attempt to check on Rolly, the maid Edna informs them that he has already departed.

Meanwhile, Lynn Marchmont experiences growing unease and dissatisfaction. She reflects on an awkward encounter with Rolly involving a request for £500 and feels a sense of boredom regarding her relatives. During lunch, Lynn argues with her mother, Adela, about the cost of hiring a gardener. Later, Lynn meets Aunt Cassie, who cryptically suggests that good news is coming but expresses concern for the mental state of Lynn's uncle, Leonard Claude. Lynn observes that Leonard has changed and suspects he may be using drugs for stimulation. Seeking clarity, Lynn decides to go for a long hike to escape the valley and contemplate her life, noting a sense of nostalgia for the order and purpose she experienced during her military service.
